description_long: This object is a vintage portable radio receiver with a rectangular body and a rounded, arching handle on top for easy carrying. The front panel features a cream-colored speaker grille, a horizontal frequency display with MW and UK bands, and several sliding or push buttons below the display. The casing is primarily a muted red-brown color with beige accents, giving it a classic mid-20th-century appearance. The sides are slim, with one side showing a circular port or knob, likely for power or tuning. The back is plain and beige, with no visible controls or openings, emphasizing its portable and self-contained design.
description: A vintage portable radio receiver with a red-brown body, cream front panel, and an arched handle, featuring a frequency display and speaker grille.
description_view_0: Front view showing the frequency display, speaker grille, buttons, and visible brand logo.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the slim profile, handle attachment, and a circular port or knob.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain beige back with no visible controls or features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the handle attachment and another circular port or knob.
